threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'train' station instead of a 'tube' station—everyone's giving you side-eye on the platform.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and saw a bloke trying to barter with the driver for a fare. Only in South London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using a broken U-Bahn bench as a stage for their rap battle outside the Karstadt. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar disguised as a laundromat—grab a drink while your clothes spin. It's a weirdly comforting vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can spot a tourist from a block away just by the way they hold their phone, snapping pics of every mural.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 19 Polk after a wedding, only to slip into a deep conversation about the meaning of life with a busstop philosopher.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just another trendy spot. Locals know the secret: it's the voices of the older residents that echo the real soul of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their bewilderment over the APCs—advanced public computers—at most metro stations, they always fumble with tickets.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'please' when asking for a bag at the corner shop. Everyone knows it’s just expected to carry your own.
Heard a couple arguing on the bus last night; she was convinced the driver was a robot because he didn’t smile once. It's the little things.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of fresh döner wafting through the air every Friday night, mixed with the sounds of street musicians, feels like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop next to an art gallery, both bustling at 3 AM. It’s the perfect blend of late-night cravings and creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot 'em when they instinctively know which taco truck has the best lengua, while the newcomers just stare at the menu.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a full tuxedo on the 49, reciting Shakespeare to everyone like it was opening night. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all Gràcia's squares are just for selfies, but locals know they’re the heartbeats of community festivals and impromptu concerts.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they order 'café con leche' in a bar—too much hesitation over whether to sit at the counter or choose a table.
